terms.definition: Within-cluster sum of squares—the total squared Euclidean distance from each point to its assigned centroid; lower values mean tighter clusters. Canonical definition in this topic.

Per-point measure s(i) = (b(i) − a(i)) / max(a(i), b(i)) comparing mean intra-cluster distance a(i) to mean nearest-cluster distance b(i); overall score is the mean across points in [-1, 1]. Canonical definition in this topic.
Heuristic for choosing k: plot inertia vs number of clusters and look for a bend where marginal reduction in inertia slows sharply.
Chance-corrected measure of agreement between two partitions (cluster labels vs ground truth); 1 is perfect match, 0 is chance-level. Canonical external metric definition in this topic.
Mutual information between cluster assignments and reference labels, normalized to [0, 1]; higher means more shared information between partitions.
External cluster metric: each cluster contains only members of a single ground-truth class (clusters are pure).
External cluster metric: all members of each ground-truth class are assigned to the same cluster (classes are fully recovered).
